schemas.schema_prompt:
Generate realistic synthetic US banking data with strict referential integrity across all tables and valid primary/foreign keys.  
Follow all DDL constraints exactly (data types, nullability, lengths, uniqueness) and keep dates/timestamps logically consistent with no future dates.  
Maintain business consistency: balances and amounts plausible, `OUTSTANDING_AMOUNT <= PRINCIPAL_AMOUNT`, and `PAYMENT_AMOUNT = principal + interest + penalty`.  
Use realistic distributions (account/customer statuses, transaction types/channels), mostly USD, and mark about 2% transactions as suspicious.  
Create diverse, non-duplicate, test-ready records with coherent customer-account-loan-payment relationships.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE CUSTOMERS (
  CUSTOMER_ID BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,
  FIRST_NAME VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  LAST_NAME VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  DATE_OF_BIRTH DATE NOT NULL,
  EMAIL VARCHAR(120) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  PHONE VARCHAR(20),
  STREET_ADDRESS VARCHAR(120),
  CITY VARCHAR(60),
  STATE_CODE CHAR(2),
  POSTAL_CODE VARCHAR(10),
  KYC_STATUS VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  RISK_SCORE DECIMAL(5,2),
  CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  UPDATED_AT TIMESTAMP
);
schema_list.instructions:
Generate adult customers (age 18-90), realistic US names/addresses, unique email.
KYC_STATUS in {VERIFIED,PENDING,REJECTED} with 80/15/5 distribution.
RISK_SCORE between 0 and 100 with 2 decimals.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE ACCOUNTS (
  ACCOUNT_ID BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,
  CUSTOMER_ID BIGINT NOT NULL,
  ACCOUNT_NUMBER VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  ACCOUNT_TYPE VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  CURRENCY_CODE CHAR(3) NOT NULL,
  BALANCE DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
  AVAILABLE_BALANCE DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
  STATUS VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  OPENED_DATE DATE NOT NULL,
  CLOSED_DATE DATE,
  BRANCH_CODE VARCHAR(20),
  OVERDRAFT_LIMIT DECIMAL(18,2),
  INTEREST_RATE DECIMAL(5,3),
  CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  UPDATED_AT TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (CUSTOMER_ID) REFERENCES CUSTOMERS(CUSTOMER_ID)
);
schema_list.instructions:
Every CUSTOMER_ID must exist in CUSTOMERS.
ACCOUNT_TYPE in {CHECKING,SAVINGS} with 70/30 split.
STATUS in {ACTIVE,DORMANT,CLOSED} with 85/10/5 split.
AVAILABLE_BALANCE <= BALANCE + OVERDRAFT_LIMIT.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE TRANSACTIONS (
  TRANSACTION_ID BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,
  ACCOUNT_ID BIGINT NOT NULL,
  TRANSACTION_TS TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  VALUE_DATE DATE NOT NULL,
  TRANSACTION_TYPE VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  CHANNEL VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  AMOUNT DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
  CURRENCY_CODE CHAR(3) NOT NULL,
  DESCRIPTION VARCHAR(200),
  MERCHANT_NAME VARCHAR(120),
  MERCHANT_CATEGORY VARCHAR(60),
  COUNTERPARTY_ACCOUNT VARCHAR(34),
  STATUS VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  IS_SUSPICIOUS BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
  REFERENCE_CODE VARCHAR(40) UNIQUE,
  FOREIGN KEY (ACCOUNT_ID) REFERENCES ACCOUNTS(ACCOUNT_ID)
);
schema_list.instructions:
Every ACCOUNT_ID must exist in ACCOUNTS.
TRANSACTION_TYPE in {DEBIT,CREDIT,TRANSFER,FEE,INTEREST}.
AMOUNT > 0 with right-skew (many small, few large).
2% rows IS_SUSPICIOUS = true.
VALUE_DATE should match or be 0-2 days after TRANSACTION_TS date.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE LOANS (
  LOAN_ID BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,
  CUSTOMER_ID BIGINT NOT NULL,
  ACCOUNT_ID BIGINT,
  LOAN_TYPE VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  PRINCIPAL_AMOUNT DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
  OUTSTANDING_AMOUNT DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
  INTEREST_RATE DECIMAL(5,3) NOT NULL,
  TERM_MONTHS INT NOT NULL,
  START_DATE DATE NOT NULL,
  MATURITY_DATE DATE NOT NULL,
  STATUS VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  COLLATERAL_TYPE VARCHAR(40),
  CREDIT_SCORE INT,
  CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  UPDATED_AT TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (CUSTOMER_ID) REFERENCES CUSTOMERS(CUSTOMER_ID),
  FOREIGN KEY (ACCOUNT_ID) REFERENCES ACCOUNTS(ACCOUNT_ID)
);
schema_list.instructions:
Every CUSTOMER_ID must exist in CUSTOMERS.
If ACCOUNT_ID is present, it must exist in ACCOUNTS and belong to same CUSTOMER_ID.
LOAN_TYPE in {PERSONAL,HOME,AUTO,SME}.
OUTSTANDING_AMOUNT between 0 and PRINCIPAL_AMOUNT.
Maturity date must be after start date by TERM_MONTHS.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE LOAN_PAYMENTS (
  PAYMENT_ID BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,
  LOAN_ID BIGINT NOT NULL,
  ACCOUNT_ID BIGINT NOT NULL,
  PAYMENT_TS TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  DUE_DATE DATE NOT NULL,
  PAID_DATE DATE,
  PAYMENT_AMOUNT DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
  PRINCIPAL_COMPONENT DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
  INTEREST_COMPONENT DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
  PENALTY_COMPONENT DECIMAL(18,2) NOT NULL,
  PAYMENT_METHOD VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  PAYMENT_STATUS VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  RECEIPT_NUMBER VARCHAR(40) UNIQUE,
  CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (LOAN_ID) REFERENCES LOANS(LOAN_ID),
  FOREIGN KEY (ACCOUNT_ID) REFERENCES ACCOUNTS(ACCOUNT_ID)
);
schema_list.instructions:
Every LOAN_ID must exist in LOANS; ACCOUNT_ID must exist in ACCOUNTS.
PAYMENT_AMOUNT = PRINCIPAL_COMPONENT + INTEREST_COMPONENT + PENALTY_COMPONENT.
PAYMENT_STATUS in {PAID,PENDING,FAILED,LATE}.
If status=PAID then PAID_DATE is not null; otherwise PAID_DATE may be null.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE INSU_CUSTOMER (
  CUSTOMER_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
  CUSTOMER_TYPE STRING,
  FIRST_NAME STRING,
  LAST_NAME STRING,
  GENDER STRING,
  BUSINESS_NAME STRING,
  DOB DATE,
  EMAIL STRING,
  PHONE STRING,
  COUNTRY STRING,
  STATE STRING,
  CITY STRING,
  ADDRESS_LINE1 STRING,
  ADDRESS_LINE2 STRING,
  POSTAL_CODE STRING,
  REGISTRATION_DATE DATE,
  CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP_NTZ
);
schema_list.instructions:
Purpose: Master records for insurance customers (individuals or businesses).
    customer_id: generate UUID string; must be unique.
    customer_type: choose from ['INDIVIDUAL','BUSINESS'] with distribution ~85/15.
    gender: if customer_type='INDIVIDUAL', choose from ['MALE','FEMALE','OTHER'] with distribution ~49/49/1; if 'Business', NULL.
    first_name/last_name: use faker names only when customer_type='INDIVIDUAL'; else 'NOT APPLICABLE'.
    business_name: faker.company() only when customer_type='BUSINESS'; else 'NOT APPLICABLE'.
    dob: if customer_type='INDIVIDUAL', random date between 1960-01-01 and 2005-12-31 (adults); if 'Business', NULL.
    email: faker.email(); ~100% populated; prefer global uniqueness.
    phone: faker.phone_number(); ~100% populated.
    country: pick from ['US','IN','AE','UK','CA'] with distribution ~50/20/10/10/10; state/city/postal coherent to country.
    address_line1: faker.street_address(); address_line2.
    registration_date: date between 2015-01-01 and current_date.
    created_at: datetime between registration_date and now (ensure created_at >= registration_date).

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE INSU_AGENT (
  AGENT_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
  AGENT_CODE STRING,
  FIRST_NAME STRING,
  LAST_NAME STRING,
  EMAIL STRING,
  PHONE STRING,
  LICENSE_NO STRING,
  COUNTRY STRING,
  STATE STRING,
  CITY STRING,
  STATUS STRING,
  HIRE_DATE DATE,
  CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP_NTZ
);
schema_list.instructions:
Purpose: Master records for licensed agents/brokers.
    agent_id: UUID; unique.
    agent_code: string like 'AGT' + 7–9 digits; must be unique.
    first_name/last_name: faker names.
    email: faker.email(); ~100% populated; unique-ish.
    phone: faker.phone_number(); ~100% populated.
    license_no: string like 'LIC' + 8–10 digits; ~98% unique.
    country/state/city: coherent geography; bias toward ['US','IN','UK'].
    status: choose from ['ACTIVE','INACTIVE'] with ~85/15.
    hire_date: between 2010-01-01 and today.
    created_at: datetime between hire_date and now.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE DATAGEN.INSURANCE.INSU_POLICY (
	POLICY_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
	POLICY_NUMBER STRING,
	CUSTOMER_ID STRING,
	PRODUCT_ID STRING,
	AGENT_ID STRING,
	STATUS STRING,
	EFFECTIVE_DATE DATE,
	EXPIRATION_DATE DATE,
	PREMIUM_BILLING_FREQ STRING,
	PREMIUM_AMOUNT NUMBER(14,2),
	CURRENCY STRING,
	CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP_NTZ,
	primary key (POLICY_ID),
	foreign key (CUSTOMER_ID) references INSU_CUSTOMER(CUSTOMER_ID),
	foreign key (PRODUCT_ID) references INSU_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID),
	foreign key (AGENT_ID) references INSU_AGENT(AGENT_ID)
);
schema_list.instructions:
POLICY_ID → Generate uuid4(); must be unique.
POLICY_NUMBER → Unique string like PL{YYYY}{8–10 digits} (e.g., PL2023 001829374).
CUSTOMER_ID → Pick an existing INSU_CUSTOMER.CUSTOMER_ID (NOT NULL).
PRODUCT_ID → Pick an existing INSU_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID, enforcing policy_type mix Auto/Home/Life/Health = 40%/25%/20%/15% via the product.
AGENT_ID → Pick an existing INSU_AGENT.AGENT_ID ~90% of the time, else NULL (direct/online sale).
STATUS → One of ['ACTIVE','LAPSED','CANCELED','EXPIRED'] with ~70% Active (rest distributed).
EFFECTIVE_DATE → Random date between 2016-01-01 and today.
EXPIRATION_DATE → EFFECTIVE_DATE + 1 year ± 60 days (ensure >= EFFECTIVE_DATE).
PREMIUM_BILLING_FREQ → One of ['MONTHLY','QUARTERLY','ANNUAL'] with ~70% Monthly (20% Quarterly, 10% Annual).
PREMIUM_AMOUNT → Annual premium > 0; suggest ranges by policy_type (Auto 300–2000, Home 500–3000, Life 200–5000, Health 200–4000) with ±15% noise; round 2 decimals.
CURRENCY → Choose from ['USD','INR','AED','GBP','CAD'], default from customer country 80–90% of time (10–20% cross-currency noise).
CREATED_AT → Timestamp between EFFECTIVE_DATE and LEAST(EXPIRATION_DATE, NOW()).

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE INSU_RISK_OBJECT (
  OBJECT_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
  POLICY_ID STRING,
  OBJECT_TYPE STRING,
  DESCRIPTION STRING,
  YEAR_MADE NUMBER(4,0),
  SERIAL_NO STRING,
  ADDRESS_LINE1 STRING,
  ADDRESS_LINE2 STRING,
  CITY STRING,
  STATE STRING,
  POSTAL_CODE STRING,
  COUNTRY STRING,
  SUM_INSURED NUMBER(14,2),
  CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP_NTZ,
  FOREIGN KEY (POLICY_ID) REFERENCES INSU_POLICY(POLICY_ID)
);
schema_list.instructions:
Purpose: Insured risk objects linked to policies (vehicles, properties, persons, equipment).
    object_id: UUID; unique.
    policy_id: FK to INSU_POLICY.policy_id; ~1–2 risk objects per policy on average to reach target rows.
    object_type: choose from ['VEHICLE','PROPERTY','PERSON','EQUIPMENT'] with ~50/35/10/5.
    description: faker.sentence(4–10 words).
    year_made: for Vehicle/Equipment, integer between 1980 and 2025;
    serial_no: ~100% populated for VEHICLE/PROPERTY;
    address_line1/address_line2/city/state/postal_code/country: mostly for Property (~80% filled when object_type='PROPERTY'); for other types, fill ~20–30% only.
    sum_insured: if available, pick between linked product.coverage_min and product.coverage_max; otherwise 20000–2000000.
    created_at: datetime between policy.effective_date and policy.expiration_date (or now if expiration in past).

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE INSU_CLAIM (
  CLAIM_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
  CLAIM_NUMBER STRING,
  POLICY_ID STRING,
  CUSTOMER_ID STRING,
  CLAIM_TYPE STRING,
  INCIDENT_DATE DATE,
  REPORTED_DATE DATE,
  STATUS STRING,
  LOSS_ESTIMATE NUMBER(14,2),
  DEDUCTIBLE NUMBER(14,2),
  APPROVED_AMOUNT NUMBER(14,2),
  CLOSED_DATE DATE,
  CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP_NTZ,
  FOREIGN KEY (POLICY_ID) REFERENCES INSU_POLICY(POLICY_ID),
  FOREIGN KEY (CUSTOMER_ID) REFERENCES INSU_CUSTOMER(CUSTOMER_ID)
);
schema_list.instructions:
Purpose: Claims raised against policies.
    claim_id: UUID; unique.
    claim_number: string like 'CL' + 9–12 digits; must be unique.
    policy_id: FK to INSU_POLICY.policy_id.
    customer_id: FK to INSU_CUSTOMER.customer_id (should match policy.customer_id).
    claim_type: choose from ['ACCIDENT','THEFT','FIRE','MEDICAL','NATURAL DISASTER','LIABILITY','OTHERS'] with ~45/10/8/15/7/10/5.
    incident_date: date between policy.effective_date and min(policy.expiration_date, today).
    reported_date: incident_date + 0–30 days (>= incident_date).
    status: choose from ['OPEN','IN-REVIEW','APPROVED','REJECTED','CLOSED','WITHDRAWN'] with ~25/20/25/10/18/2.
    loss_estimate: 500–500000 with long-tail; ensure >= 0.
    deductible: 0–5000; often <= product.deductible_max if accessible.
    approved_amount: if status in ['Approved','Closed'] then 50%–100% of loss_estimate; if 'Rejected' then 0; if others then NULL.
    closed_date: only when status='Closed'; pick between reported_date and reported_date + 365 days; else NULL.
    created_at: datetime is now.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE INSU_PAYMENT (
  PAYMENT_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
  POLICY_ID STRING,
  CLAIM_ID STRING,
  DIRECTION STRING,
  STATUS STRING,
  METHOD STRING NOT NULL,
  AMOUNT NUMBER(14,2),
  CURRENCY STRING,
  PAYMENT_DATE DATE,
  TRANSACTION_REF STRING,
  CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP_NTZ,
  FOREIGN KEY (POLICY_ID) REFERENCES INSU_POLICY(POLICY_ID),
  FOREIGN KEY (CLAIM_ID) REFERENCES INSU_CLAIM(CLAIM_ID)
);
schema_list.instructions:
Purpose: Monetary transactions for policies and claims (premiums, payouts, refunds).
    payment_id: UUID; unique.
    policy_id: FK to INSU_POLICY.policy_id.
    claim_id: optional FK to INSU_CLAIM.claim_id; present for payouts (~30%), NULL for premium payments (~65%) and policy refunds (~5%).
    direction: choose from ['Premium','Payout','Refund'] with ~65/30/5 (must align with claim_id presence).
    status: choose from ['Completed','Pending','Failed'] with ~90/7/3.
    method: choose from ['ACH','Card','Check','Wire','Cash','UPI'] with ~35/30/10/15/5/5.
    amount: > 0; random amount from  policy.premium_amount ±20% 
    currency: pick based on policy/customer country mapping ['USD','INR','AED','GBP','CAD'].
    payment_date: if claim_id present, between claim.reported_date and (claim.closed_date or claim.reported_date + 60 days); else within [policy.effective_date .. policy.expiration_date].
    transaction_ref: string like 'TX' + 10–14 digits; mostly unique.
    created_at: datetime between payment_date and now.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE BIAN_PARTY (
    PARTY_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
    FULL_NAME STRING,
    DATE_OF_BIRTH DATE,
    GENDER STRING,
    NATIONALITY STRING,
    SSN STRING,
    EMAIL STRING,
    PHONE_NUMBER STRING,
    ADDRESS_LINE1 STRING,
    ADDRESS_LINE2 STRING,
    CITY STRING,
    STATE STRING,
    POSTAL_CODE STRING,
    COUNTRY STRING,
    CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP
);
schema_list.instructions:
PARTY_ID → Generate UUID; must be unique.
FULL_NAME → Faker full name.
DATE_OF_BIRTH → Random date between 1970-01-01 and 1990-12-31.
GENDER → Choose from ['MALE','FEMALE','OTHER'] with ~70/29/1 distribution.
NATIONALITY → Choose from ['US','IN','AE','UK','CA'] with ~50/20/10/10/10.
SSN → Faker SSN-like string; ensure uniqueness per COUNTRY rules if needed.
EMAIL → Faker email; prefer globally unique values.
PHONE_NUMBER → Faker phone number (E.164 if you need consistency).
ADDRESS_LINE1 → Faker street address.
ADDRESS_LINE2 → Faker secondary address or empty ~40% of time.
CITY → Faker city.
STATE → Faker state/region.
POSTAL_CODE → Faker postal code.
COUNTRY → Mirror NATIONALITY distribution ['US','IN','AE','UK','CA'] ~50/20/10/10/10.
CREATED_AT → Random timestamp within last 10 years.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE BIAN_ACCOUNTS (
    ACCOUNT_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
    PARTY_ID STRING REFERENCES BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID),
    ACCOUNT_TYPE STRING,
    ACCOUNT_STATUS STRING,
    OPEN_DATE DATE,
    BALANCE NUMBER(18,2),
    CURRENCY STRING,
    INTEREST_RATE FLOAT,
    BRANCH_ID STRING,
    CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP
);
schema_list.instructions:
ACCOUNT_ID → Generate UUID; unique.
PARTY_ID → Randomly pick from existing BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID).
ACCOUNT_TYPE → 60% "Checking", 30% "Savings", 10% "Credit Card".
ACCOUNT_STATUS → 70% "Active", 20% "Dormant", 10% "Closed".
OPEN_DATE → Random date within last 15 years.
BALANCE → If ACCOUNT_TYPE="Credit Card": 20% negative (−5000 to −100), else positive (100 to 100000).
CURRENCY → Choose from ["USD","EUR","GBP","INR","AED"] weighted by PARTY.COUNTRY if available.
INTEREST_RATE → 0.1%–5% for Checking/Savings, 10%–25% for Credit Card.
BRANCH_ID → "BR" + 3–4 digit code (e.g., BR001–BR050).
CREATED_AT → Random timestamp on or after OPEN_DATE (spread up to ~10 years)

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE BIAN_TRANSACTION (
    TRANSACTION_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
    ACCOUNT_ID STRING REFERENCES BIAN_ACCOUNTS(ACCOUNT_ID),
    TRANSACTION_TYPE STRING,
    AMOUNT NUMBER(18,2),
    CURRENCY STRING,
    TRANSACTION_TIMESTAMP TIMESTAMP,
    MERCHANT_NAME STRING,
    MERCHANT_CATEGORY STRING,
    LOCATION STRING,
    CHANNEL STRING,
    STATUS STRING,
    CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP
);
schema_list.instructions:
TRANSACTION_ID → Generate UUID; unique.
ACCOUNT_ID → Randomly pick from existing BIAN_ACCOUNTS(ACCOUNT_ID).
TRANSACTION_TYPE → Choose from ["DEPOSIT","WITHDRAWAL","PAYMENT","TRANSFER","FEE","REFUND"] with realistic skew (e.g., DEPOSIT/WITHDRAWAL heavy).
AMOUNT → > 0; typical range 1–5000 (tail up to 20000 for high-value).
CURRENCY → Inherit from the linked account’s CURRENCY when possible; else pick from ["USD","EUR","GBP","INR","AED"].
TRANSACTION_TIMESTAMP → Faker datetime between 2010-01-01 and now.
MERCHANT_NAME → Faker company (blank for non-merchant types like TRANSFER/FEE).
MERCHANT_CATEGORY → Pick from ["GROCERY","RESTAURANT","FUEL","ONLINE","TRAVEL","UTILITY","OTHER"]; empty for TRANSFER/FEE.
LOCATION → City + Country for merchanted transactions; else empty.
CHANNEL → Choose from ["POS","ONLINE","MOBILE","ATM","BRANCH"].
STATUS → 92% "POSTED", 6% "PENDING", 2% "REVERSED".
CREATED_AT → Timestamp on or after TRANSACTION_TIMESTAMP (within +0–3 days).
Behavioral rules → ~80% non-fraud normal mix; ~10% “structuring” sequences: per selected ACCOUNT_ID, emit 3–5 DEPOSITs of 1000–9999 within 1–3 days; ~10% high-velocity bursts: 2–4 small PAYMENTS/WITHDRAWALS within 30–120 minutes.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE BIAN_KYC_PROFILE (
    KYC_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
    PARTY_ID STRING REFERENCES BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID),
    KYC_STATUS STRING,
    REVIEW_DATE DATE,
    RISK_LEVEL STRING,
    REVIEWER_ID STRING,
    LAST_UPDATED TIMESTAMP
);
schema_list.instructions:
KYC_ID → UUID; unique.
PARTY_ID → Random from BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID).
KYC_STATUS → 70% "Verified", 20% "Pending", 10% "Rejected".
REVIEW_DATE → Random date within last 3 years (bias to last 90 days if "Pending").
RISK_LEVEL → 25% "High", 50% "Medium", 25% "Low" (increase "High" odds when KYC_STATUS="Rejected").
REVIEWER_ID → From pool EMP1001–EMP1020.
LAST_UPDATED → Timestamp on or after REVIEW_DATE (very recent if "Pending").

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE BIAN_DOCUMENT (
    DOC_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
    PARTY_ID STRING REFERENCES BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID),
    DOC_TYPE STRING,
    DOC_NUMBER STRING,
    ISSUE_DATE DATE,
    EXPIRY_DATE DATE,
    ISSUING_AUTHORITY STRING,
    DOC_STATUS STRING,
    UPLOADED_AT TIMESTAMP
);
schema_list.instructions:
DOC_ID → UUID; unique.
PARTY_ID → Random from BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID).
DOC_TYPE → 50% "Passport", 30% "Driver License", 20% "National ID".
DOC_NUMBER → Pattern by DOC_TYPE (e.g., Passport: 2 letters + 7 digits; Driver License: alnum 8–12; National ID: digits 10–12).
ISSUE_DATE → Random date within last 15 years.
EXPIRY_DATE → After ISSUE_DATE: 5–10 years for Passport/Driver License, ~10 years for National ID.
ISSUING_AUTHORITY → From ["Passport Office","DMV","National ID Authority"] by DOC_TYPE.
DOC_STATUS → 85% "Active", 15% "Expired" (force "Expired" if EXPIRY_DATE < today).
UPLOADED_AT → Timestamp within ±7 days of ISSUE_DATE.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE BIAN_RISK_ASSESSMENT (
    RISK_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
    PARTY_ID STRING REFERENCES BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID),
    RISK_MODEL_NAME STRING,
    RISK_SCORE FLOAT,
    RISK_CATEGORY STRING,
    REVIEWED_BY STRING,
    REVIEW_DATE DATE,
    RECOMMENDATION STRING,
    CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP
);
schema_list.instructions:
RISK_ID → UUID; unique.
PARTY_ID → Random from BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID).
RISK_MODEL_NAME → Choose from ["MODEL_A","MODEL_B","MODEL_C"].
RISK_SCORE → Float 0–100 with 20% > 80 (high-risk).
RISK_CATEGORY → Derive: "High" if >80, "Medium" if 40–80, "Low" if <40.
REVIEWED_BY → EMP2001–EMP2020.
REVIEW_DATE → Date within last 2 years.
RECOMMENDATION → From ["Enhanced Due Diligence","Monitor","Proceed"] based on RISK_CATEGORY.
CREATED_AT → Timestamp on or after REVIEW_DATE.

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE BIAN_DEVICE_ACCESS (
    DEVICE_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
    PARTY_ID STRING REFERENCES BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID),
    DEVICE_TYPE STRING,
    DEVICE_OS STRING,
    IP_ADDRESS STRING,
    LOCATION STRING,
    LOGIN_TIME TIMESTAMP,
    LOGOUT_TIME TIMESTAMP,
    AUTH_METHOD STRING,
    SESSION_STATUS STRING
);
schema_list.instructions:
DEVICE_ID → UUID; unique.
PARTY_ID → Random from BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID).
DEVICE_TYPE → 60% "Mobile", 30% "Desktop", 10% "Tablet".
DEVICE_OS → By DEVICE_TYPE: Mobile → ["iOS","Android"]; Desktop → ["Windows","macOS","Linux"]; Tablet → ["iPadOS","Android"].
IP_ADDRESS → Valid IPv4 or IPv6.
LOCATION → City + Country.
LOGIN_TIME → Timestamp within last 6 months.
LOGOUT_TIME → After LOGIN_TIME (0.1–8 hours later); 5% sessions may omit LOGOUT_TIME to simulate active sessions.
AUTH_METHOD → 15% "Biometric", 50% "Password", 35% "Two-Factor".
SESSION_STATUS → "Active" if no LOGOUT_TIME else "Closed".

schema_list.ddl:
CREATE TABLE BIAN_FRAUD_ALERT (
    ALERT_ID STRING PRIMARY KEY,
    TRANSACTION_ID STRING REFERENCES BIAN_TRANSACTION(TRANSACTION_ID),
    PARTY_ID STRING REFERENCES BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID),
    ALERT_TYPE STRING,
    TRIGGERED_AT TIMESTAMP,
    RESOLUTION_STATUS STRING,
    RESOLVED_BY STRING
);
schema_list.instructions:
ALERT_ID → UUID; unique.
TRANSACTION_ID → Random from BIAN_TRANSACTION(TRANSACTION_ID).
PARTY_ID → Random from BIAN_PARTY(PARTY_ID) (ideally matching the party behind the linked account).
ALERT_TYPE → Choose from ["Unusual Location","Multiple Failed Logins","High-Value Transaction","Suspicious IP"].
TRIGGERED_AT → Timestamp within last 1 year.
RESOLUTION_STATUS → 60% "Open", 30% "Resolved", 10% "Escalated".
RESOLVED_BY → If RESOLUTION_STATUS in ("Resolved","Escalated"): EMP3001–EMP3050 else NULL.
